steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what researchers did to a "shortcoming" (the myopic geographic focus) after gaining new access to banking records. The word must fit a structure where new data allows researchers to act on — specifically, reduce or address — a prior limitation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘mitigate’: To mitigate means to lessen or reduce something harmful. Gaining new data directly reduced the shortcoming's impact, so this fits the sentence perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘presuppose’: To presuppose means to assume something in advance. Researchers cannot presuppose a shortcoming after gaining data — the timeline is backward, and presupposing a limitation is not something data enables. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘validate’: To validate means to confirm or endorse. Researchers would validate findings or methods, not a shortcoming — and gaining new data opposes, rather than endorses, the limitation. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘categorize’: To categorize means to sort into groups. There is no logical connection between acquiring banking records and classifying a research shortcoming. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
